summary: mintClientCredentials sends grant_type only via the token_url query string with an empty POST body; expires_in is parsed via cliutil.ExtractInt (string-or-number) instead of a bare int struct field.
reason: Confirmed via live testing against FINRA's real FIP OAuth2 token endpoint: a form-encoded body (even with a matching grant_type value) causes FINRA to reject the request with {"error":"unsupported_grant_type"}; the generic OAuth2 client_credentials template sends both a query-string grant_type and a form body per RFC 6749 3.2, which FINRA's non-standard implementation rejects. Separately, FINRA returns expires_in as a JSON string (e.g. "43170"), not a number, which crashed the generic template's typed int field.
validated_outcome: Live token mint against https://api.finra.org succeeded after both fixes; confirmed end-to-end with real production data across 6 commands.
